math.LO.summary: Modal stays the event-level baseline; type-theory, set theory, forcing, and definability form the bridge-level ring around it.

modal_baseline.label: modal baseline
modal_baseline.summary: The single event-level LO baseline that stays on the mainline.

bridge_ring.label: bridge-level ring
bridge_ring.summary: Stable LO bridge ring: type-theory contrast, set-theory inner backbone, forcing outer branch, definability capstone.

boundary_negative.label: boundary negatives
boundary_negative.summary: Boundary negatives that keep the LO ring interpretable instead of absorbing nearby set-theory, modal, or definability neighbors.

review_boundary.label: review near-miss
review_boundary.summary: Near-miss cases close to the LO ring that remain review-only.

case_registry.narrative_note: Current LO event-level baseline; graph-supported and replay-backed.
Type-theory contrast branch: lexical bridge only, without graph/event support.
Set-theory inner backbone: strongest graph-supported LO bridge branch below event-level.
Forcing outer branch: outward bridge into the large-cardinal/forcing neighborhood, not an event edge.
Definability capstone: narrow branch that closes the forcing/cardinal neighborhood into set-theoretic semantics.
Keeps the forcing branch from swallowing the set-theory backbone.
Shows that shared cardinal/forcing vocabulary does not license the whole set-theory cluster as a positive branch.
Definability is not a free pass without the narrower set-theoretic special-object support.
Domain-mismatch boundary for the definability branch.
Graph-near intuitionistic logic is still not type-theory without type objects.
Modal/semantics similarity should remain outside the type-theory branch.
Near-miss on the forcing outer branch; keep review-only until the branch boundary changes.
Near-miss inside the set-theory/forcing neighborhood; visible as a review boundary, not as a positive branch.

math.AG.summary: AG keeps one confirmed core in the baseline topology and exposes the bridge ring / excluded boundary as metadata-only narrative support.

confirmed_core.label: confirmed core
confirmed_core.summary: Runner-confirmed event-level core for the current AG mainline.

bridge_ring.label: bridge ring
bridge_ring.summary: Semantic bridge-level positives worth showing around the core, but not promoted by the current gate or runner.

excluded_boundary.label: excluded boundary
excluded_boundary.summary: Nearby negatives that keep the AG region bounded even when vocabulary or taxonomy overlap looks tempting.

review_boundary.label: review-only dispute
review_boundary.summary: Boundary dispute kept visible for expert review, but intentionally left outside the default bridge ring.

case_registry.narrative_note: 唯一稳定 runner-positive；作为 AG 主线 confirmed core 保留。
共享 curves；值得作为 bridge ring 呈现，但不通过当前 exact-term gate。
共享 generic varieties；保留为 bridge ring 的远端支撑，不升级为 runner-positive。
仅共享 sheaves (1 个对象词)；不满足当前 >=2 exact-term gate
class-overlap-only false positive；无 exact shared AG object terms
domain boundary negative；凸几何主题不应进入 math.AG object continuity
Review-only AG boundary dispute; keep visible in metadata, not inside the default bridge ring.

math.CO.summary: CO stays outside the baseline topology; export metadata only exposes the landed matroid MVP, its supporting bridge, and its boundary cases.

matroid_mvp.label: matroid MVP
matroid_mvp.summary: The landed matroid-family MVP contract. This is selected rule metadata, not baseline graph truth.

bridge_support.label: supporting bridge
bridge_support.summary: Graph-facing matroid support kept outside the current MVP gate.

excluded_boundary.label: excluded boundary
excluded_boundary.summary: Boundary negatives that keep the matroid MVP conservative.

review_boundary.label: review near-miss
review_boundary.summary: Near-miss cases parked outside the matroid MVP until a broader rule exists.

deferred_branch.label: deferred branch
deferred_branch.summary: Documented bridge branch deferred from the first landed rule.

case_registry.narrative_note: Deferred random/percolation bridge branch; documented, but not the landed first-rule contract.
Supporting matroid bridge positive kept outside the landed MVP gate.
Primary landed matroid MVP positive.

Generic graph-vocabulary boundary that must stay outside the matroid branch.
Polytope-only near-miss that stays outside the first matroid rule.

edges.narrative_note: Second landed matroid MVP positive inside the selected contract.
Supporting matroid bridge positive kept outside the landed MVP gate.

math.DS.summary: DS stays outside the baseline topology, but its ergodic/entropy skeleton now has a graph-visible non-baseline subgraph with bridge, boundary, and review cases.

ergodic_entropy_skeleton.label: ergodic/entropy skeleton
ergodic_entropy_skeleton.summary: Bridge-level DS skeleton around ergodic/entropy continuity; graph-facing metadata only, not baseline topology.

excluded_boundary.label: excluded boundary
excluded_boundary.summary: Boundary negatives that keep broad topological or measure overlap from becoming DS continuity truth.

review_boundary.label: review boundary
review_boundary.summary: Near-miss case kept review-only while the DS ergodic/fractal boundary remains unsettled.

case_registry.narrative_note: Bridge-level DS positive inside the ergodic/entropy neighborhood; stable enough for metadata, not for baseline topology.

math.NA.summary: NA contributes a bridge-level Krylov iterative skeleton plus boundary/review metadata; no NA nodes or baseline EVOLVES_TO edges are exported yet.

krylov_iterative_skeleton.label: krylov iterative skeleton
krylov_iterative_skeleton.summary: Bridge-level NA skeleton around Krylov/GMRES continuity; graph-facing metadata only until NA has a real event-level or runner-ready baseline.

excluded_boundary.label: excluded boundary
excluded_boundary.summary: Boundary negatives that keep broad approximation or convergence overlap from becoming NA continuity truth.

review_boundary.label: review boundary
review_boundary.summary: Near-miss case kept review-only while the Krylov-to-application boundary remains unsettled.
